title: Teaching
text:
Be Bayesian my Friend (2h)

Introductory workshop on Bayesian inference, covering the theorem, posterior distributions and credible intervals, hierarchical models, and hands-on modelling with brms.

Be Bayesian my Friend 2.0 (20h)

A comprehensive four-part introduction to Bayesian inference, from priors and MCMC to hierarchical and spatial models, using JAGS, brms, INLA and inlabru, with real-world health applications throughout.

Bayesian Inference Using R-INLA (10h)

Workshop on fitting latent Gaussian models with INLA and inlabru, from fundamentals to spatial modelling, disease mapping and geostatistics, through six hands-on lessons with real datasets.

title: Joaquín Martínez-Minaya

     CV
  

  
  

Associate Professor in Statistics and Optimization

Dept. of Applied Statistics, Operations Research and Quality · Universitat Politècnica de València
My name is Joaquín Martínez-Minaya, and I am an Associate Professor in the Department of Applied Statistics and Operational Research and Quality at the Universitat Politècnica de València (Spain). My research lies in the field of Applied and Computational Bayesian Statistics, where I develop statistical methods for the analysis of multivariate, compositional, spatial and spatio-temporal data. My work combines methodological developments in Bayesian inference with applications in environmental health, ecology and biomedicine. I am interested in hierarchical models, latent Gaussian models, compositional data analysis, measurement agreement, model assessment, validation and predictive performance, and species distribution modelling. From a computational perspective, my research relies on Bayesian inference techniques, especially Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation (INLA) and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo methods.



Spatio-temporal Models Compositional Data Analysis Bayesian Computational Methods Bayesian Deep Learning Mixed & Hierarchical Models Species Distribution Modelling Environmental Health Biomedicine
